evidences.title: Extract Historical Data from Binance Using Python
evidences.content: ``` symbol = 'BTCUSDT' 'BTCUSDT' ``` We can set a custom start and end time for our function by parsing the date to date time format. ``` # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second)start_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0) # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) 2024 3 15 0 0 0 2024 6 15 0 0 0 ``` `datetime.datetime`: Creates datetime object for specified start and end times. ### 4. Fetching Historical Klines Data Now, we use the `get_historical_klines` main function to extract historical candlestick data for our custom time range. ``` klines = client.get_historical_klines( symbol=symbol, interval=Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_str=str(start_time), end_str=str(end_time)) str str ``` [...] `interval`: Time interval it's set to `Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE` for 1-minute intervals. You can use `Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_5MINUTE` for 5 minutes, `Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1HOUR` for 1 hour intervals etc. > Note: We did not set `limit` argument as it is equal to 1000 by default. You can set it to any desired number up to 1000. The question you might ask: What if I want to fetch more than 1000 data points? Do...

evidences.title: Save Historical data from Binance
evidences.content: ``` frombinance.client import Client print(interval_to_milliseconds(Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE)) print(interval_to_milliseconds(Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE)) print(interval_to_milliseconds(KLINE_INTERVAL_1WEEK)) ``` ### Fetch the Klines already Ok enough messing around, we’re ready to build our function to fetch historical data. With this function we have a really simple way of fetching a list of klines using simple to use dates and intervals. [...] First install the `dateparser` package ``` install ``` Now we can create a function to take a readable string into milliseconds. With this function we can convert all sorts of handy date formats to milliseconds like the examples below. ``` print(date_to_milliseconds("January 01, 2018")) print(date_to_milliseconds("11 hours ago UTC")) print(date_to_milliseconds("now UTC")) ``` ### Binance Kline endpoint Now we have that out of the way we can start to work with the Binance API. For our purposes we are interested in the get\_klines endpoint to fetch the actual data. This takes parameters `symbol` - e.g ETHBTC `interval` - one of (1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w, 1M) `limit` - max 500 `startTime` - milli...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: Scheduled changes: The All Book Tickers stream (!bookTicker) is set to be removed in late November 2022. Please use the Individual Book Ticker Streams instead. (@bookTicker). Multiple @bookTicker streams can be subscribed to over one connection. For example: wss://stream.binance.us:9443/stream?streams=btcusdt@bookTicker/bnbbtc@bookTicker Market Data: Added a new optional parameter type in two endpoints: GET /api/v3/ticker and GET /api/v3/ticker/24hr. Also removed Individual Symbol Ticker Streams as it duplicates with Ticker Order Book Stream, supported new candlestick chart interval: 1s. Get Asset Distribution History endpoint: Updated this endpoint to also query the rebate distribution record. [...] If fromId, startTime, and endTime are not sent, the most recent aggregate trades will be returned. Data Source: Database ### Get Order Book Depth > Example ``` -X "GET"" ``` ``` import requests resp = requests. get(' print(resp. json()) ``` > Response ``` {" lastUpdateId ": 1027024, " bids ":, " asks ":} ``` `GET /api/v3/depth` Use this endpoint to get order book depth (prices and quantities of bids and asks). evidences.title: Binance Data
evidences.content: - Symbol - The symbol for which the timeseries data refers - Open - This is the opening price of the time period - High - This is the highest price of the time period - Low - This is the lowest price of the time period - Close - This is the closing price of the time period - Volume (Crypto) - This is the volume in the transacted Ccy. Ie. For BTC/USDT, this is in BTC amount - Volume Base Ccy - This is the volume in the base/converted ccy. Ie. For BTC/USDT, this is in USDT amount - Trade Count - This is the unique number of trades for the given time period [...] - High - This is the highest price of the tick period - Low - This is the lowest price of the tick period - Close - This is the closing price of the tick period (last transaction) - Volume - This is the volume in the transacted Ccy. Ie. For BTC/USDT, this is in BTC amount - Dollar Volume - This is the volume in USD(T) - Tick Size - Tick count of the OHLC window ETHUSDT Tick Sizes - SAMPLES: 610 1500 4500 Upgrade to access full resource files [...] - Open - This is the opening price of the time period - High - This is the highest price of the time period - Low - This is the lowest price of the time period - Close - This is...

evidences.title: Updates on Tick Size for New ETHUSDT Options Contracts | Binance announcement,Binance news
evidences.content: # Updates on Tick Size for New ETHUSDT Options Contracts. In order to increase market liquidity and improve trading experience, Binance will adjust the tick size (i.e. the minimum change in the unit price) **from 0.1 to 0.2 for all new ETHUSDT Options** **contracts** listed after **2024-10-16 08:00 (UTC)**. * This adjustment will **not** impact any Options trading operations. * The tick size of existing ETHUSDT Options contracts that were previously listed will remain unchanged at 0.1 until expiration. * Only **ETHUSDT Options** contracts listed after **2024-10-16 08:00 (UTC)** will be adjusted to the new tick size of 0.2. * API users may use *GET /eapi/v1/exchangeInfo* for the latest tick size of ETHUSDT Options contracts. Please refer to Binance Options Contract Specifications for more details on contract specifications and adjust your trading strategy according to the change to avoid unnecessary impact on your trading. Please refer to the original English version for the most accurate information, in case any discrepancies arise. Trade on-the-go with Binance’s crypto trading app (iOS/Android). *Binance reserves the right in its sole discretion to amend or cancel this announce...
